question: The historiographical debate about Caribbean slavery centres on whether
options: It was primarily a racial system or an economic one — Eric Williams versus traditional racial interpretations
Temporary escape from the plantation, and grand marronage referred to permanent flight
Eric Williams of Trinidad and Tobago, breaking with US pressure to isolate Cuba
The region had no welfare state and relied entirely on monocrop agriculture, making it catastrophically vulnerable
answer: It was primarily a racial system or an economic one — Eric Williams versus traditional racial interpretations

question: The Abolitionist Sambo stereotype was used by pro-slavery advocates to argue
options: British abolitionism was partly driven by metropolitan political concerns rather than purely moral ones
That enslaved Africans were childlike and incapable of self-governance
It was primarily a racial system or an economic one — Eric Williams versus traditional racial interpretations
The infamous pirate capital of Jamaica, seen by contemporaries as divine punishment
answer: That enslaved Africans were childlike and incapable of self-governance
